title: Post-implementation review + SYSTEM EVALUATION #1 — Clotho slice 4b
note: ADVISORY evaluation anchored to commit-verified facts. First retrospective produced by the full lifecycle (pre-review -> entry ritual GRANTED -> harvest -> verify -> enroll -> this).
run: clotho slice 4b (harvest merge git:af64b88 of origin/clotho-task4b head git:15de463; PR #126)
what_was_built: test/doc/ledger weavers (clotho/weavers/{test,doc,ledger}.mjs + util additions) with isolating oracles (test-weaver-{test,doc,ledger}.mjs, incl. 6 gauntlet-found coverage gaps and the H1/H2 council hard-stop resolutions); D25 + D31 and their contracts flipped SPECIFIED-PENDING-IMPLEMENTATION -> NORMATIVE-CURRENT with passing oracles
verification_outcome: clotho npm test: 10 tests green (7 -> 10); verify-contracts 188/188 (flipped records pass the NORMATIVE-has-oracle discipline); build-gate regression green; entry ritual 13/13 GRANTED before any work
what_worked: HARVEST-FIRST paid exactly as the loadout review predicted: 13 files / +949 landed by one clean merge with lineage preserved — zero reimplementation
the documentation-first substrate worked end-to-end: the frozen records named their oracles in advance, the harvest satisfied them, and the flip to NORMATIVE was mechanical
the pre-review + entry ritual cost minutes and front-loaded the two traps (D25 provenance split, D31 independence) the queries encode
keeping OUR readdirSync verifier sweep meant the 4b-era hand-list defect never landed
defects_found_during: the original substrate's becomes_normative_when named test-test.mjs / test-ledger-weaver.mjs while the implementation shipped test-weaver-{test,ledger}.mjs — the harvest's own record flips resolved the drift; lesson: name oracles by final filename in future substrates
process_mistakes_preserved: mutation-tested an UNCOMMITTED file and reverted with git checkout — which restored the last committed version, wiping the legitimate uncommitted edits along with the mutation (caught by the verifier staying red at 202/203) -> rule: commit or stash before mutation tests, or revert the mutation with a targeted counter-edit, never a whole-file checkout

redundancy: none deleted this slice: the weavers share clotho/weavers/util.mjs (single D33 scanner) as designed — no duplicated scanner logic found
WATCH: doc.mjs and ledger.mjs each split canonical Markdown (D31 requires the ledger's OWN counted split — the duplication is a frozen decision, not accidental redundancy; do not 'fold' it)

candidate: examples/ now carries reader-4b-{argo,correct,hallucinating} plus the slice's committed implementer artifact — one worked pair per module is the convention; prune extras at slice-7 documentation pass

process_dependencies.slice_5_needs: 4b weavers (query.mjs reads their products; the complete-weave driver orchestrates git+code+test+doc+ledger)
process_dependencies.slice_6_needs: 5's driver (flagship acceptance runs a complete weave)
process_dependencies.slice_7_needs: 6's flagship run (reproduction evidence re-derives it from committed bytes)
process_dependencies.conclusion: slices are SEQUENTIAL at the slice level — no cross-slice concurrency available

concurrency_opportunities_via_merkle_dag: WITHIN slice 5: the five per-package weaver runs (breakout, build-gate, clotho, connectors/ai-peer-mcp, merkle-dag) are independent by construction (one-writer-per-file over disjoint package trees) — the complete-weave driver can dispatch them concurrently and merge products under the merkle-dag discipline (content-addressed nodes, Rule-3 re-derivation, controller-only settle), exactly runBuild's DAG shape

WITHIN slice 5 implementation: query.mjs and weave.mjs are separate files with separate oracles (test-query.mjs / test-weave.mjs) — buildable by parallel agents in worktrees and merged conflict-free (one writer per file)
test execution: clotho test-all is already sequential-fast (10 tests); parallelism not worth the harness complexity yet — revisit if the suite passes ~30s

optimizations_for_future_runs.optimization: name becomes_normative_when oracles by their FINAL intended filename when freezing a substrate (the 4b naming drift cost a reconciliation check)
optimizations_for_future_runs.lands_in: clotho/memory design-substrate authoring practice; applies to any future SPECIFIED-PENDING record

optimizations_for_future_runs.optimization: slice 5: implement query.mjs and weave.mjs as two parallel worktree agents (disjoint files, separate oracles), merge after both oracles pass — the merkle-dag one-writer discipline applied to our own workflow
slice 5's driver should expose per-package weave as independently-dispatchable units so the runtime concurrency (five packages) mirrors the implementation concurrency
optimizations_for_future_runs.lands_in: the slice-5 pre-review + D34 driver design
